item.title: Issue: Data binding overview - example is not present - @whitehorsesoft
item.description:
Issue description

In data binding overview page (at https://github.com/dotnet/docs-desktop/blob/main/dotnet-desktop-guide/net/wpf/data/index.md), in Data validation section, under "Associating validation rules with a binding," it states

A DataErrorValidationRule object checks for errors that are raised by objects that implement the IDataErrorInfo interface. For an example of using this validation rule, see DataErrorValidationRule.

However, an example is not present at DataErrorValidationRule documentation. It would be better to state "more information is found at..." instead of "For an example..."

.NET or .NET Framework

Check the .NET target framework(s) being used, and include the version number(s). Put an x between the [ ] to check it.

  • [x] .NET (5 or Core)
  • [x] .NET Framework

Technology

Check the .NET target framework(s) being used, and include the version number(s). Put an x between the [ ] to check it.

  • [x] WPF
  • [ ] Windows Forms

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: adcf9793-10cf-bdeb-3810-9be075db969f
  • Version Independent ID: 8cfffaa6-fa5c-a50b-0110-1750713bd153
  • Content: Data binding overview - WPF .NET
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/net/wpf/data/index.md
  • Product: dotnet-desktop
  • Technology: dotnet-wpf
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo

item.title: Issue: We should write document for Windows Forms .NET 6 right now, not .NET 5? - @donhuvy
item.description:
I learned this from ASP.NET Core docs. The ultimate of reference document is supporting developers. Current version of .NET is .NET 5, but it still in progress, why we don't write document for Windows Forms .NET 6 right now? Even with Visual Studio 2022 or Visual Studio 2019 preview with many support design-time for Windows Forms.

  • When .NET 6 release, the document of Windows Forms .NET 5 maybe finish.
  • If applying the above proposal strategy, When .NET 6 release, the document of Windows Forms .NET 6 also finish. It will be better.

item.title: Issue: Please update this information for .Net core through .Net 5+ - @davi1td
item.description:
Hi, this really needs to be updated ASAP with the notes from this winforms issue: https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/discussions/4894

attach to the "DesignToolsServer.exe" process from the instance of Visual Studio that is doing the debugging. Regards, -TD

item.title: Issue: Scrollviewer Template lacking vital details - @Krythic
item.description:
The suggested template is missing a vital property being set, which is CanContentScroll being true. I have created a Stackoverflow question and answer regarding this, located here: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67794659/customizing-scrollviewer-style-breaks-richtextbox-mouse-wheel-scrolling

Without that property set, RichTextBoxes lose their vertical mouse scrolling functionality; this is also not noted anywhere as being a problem. The style example doesn't need to be corrected, but a note should certainly be added to the page that tells people about the requirement.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 348ed980-f1c8-9b92-4fca-c6c073b8fbd1
  • Version Independent ID: 1f4c955f-034e-4433-6567-95de212c387e
  • Content: ScrollViewer Styles and Templates - WPF .NET Framework
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/framework/wpf/controls/scrollviewer-styles-and-templates.md
  • Product: dotnet-framework
  • Technology: dotnet-wpf
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo

item.title: Issue: Upgrade project(s) to the SDK-style project format - @github-actions[bot]
item.description:
There are 2 project(s) using the non-SDK-style project format. This is an auto-generated issue, detailed and discussed in dotnet/docs#22271.

  • [ ] dotnet-desktop-guide/.../snippets/how-to-iterate-through-all-nodes-of-a-windows-forms-treeview-control/cs/project.csproj
  • [ ] dotnet-desktop-guide/.../snippets/how-to-iterate-through-all-nodes-of-a-windows-forms-treeview-control/vb/project.vbproj

Consider upgrading the project(s) to the SDK-style format.

If this project is intentionally demonstrating the old project style, you can optionally configure to ignore this automated issue. Create a file at the root of the repository, named dotnet-versionsweeper.json and add an ignore entry following the globbing patterns detailed here.

{
    "ignore": [
        "**/path/to/example.csproj"
    ]
}

item.title: Issue: drag & drop: e.Data.GetDataPresent(DataFormats.StringFormat) does not return True while it should - @rubend30
item.description:
In the following step of the walkthrough: To process the dropped data the first if-statement returns false when dropping the selected text "green" on the circle.

When debugging I added the following to check if the data was included in the DataObject: var color = e.Data.GetData(DataFormats.StringFormat);

This does return the string "green" (type: System.String). Yet the next line returns false: if (e.Data.GetDataPresent(DataFormats.StringFormat))

This was a one on one copy of the steps in the walkthrough. The project is using the following target framework: <TargetFramework>net5.0-windows10.0.18362.0</TargetFramework>

Thank you in advance for your time.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 23f605d0-6326-17d5-3087-4f6ef96ab9bb
  • Version Independent ID: 10411c7d-3586-4904-6d4f-79e84f0c9b98
  • Content: Walkthrough: Enabling Drag and Drop on a User Control - WPF .NET Framework
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/framework/wpf/advanced/walkthrough-enabling-drag-and-drop-on-a-user-control.md
  • Product: dotnet-framework
  • Technology: dotnet-wpf
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo

item.title: Issue: This WPF example doesn't work , can not drag any file. is it a bug or env problem? - @AGIBuild
item.description:
Test env: windows 10 21H1(19043.985) VS 2019(16.9.6) Project: WPF empty project (.net core 5.0)

Result: Can not drag a file to the WPF program window.

Root cause: Running Visual Studio as Administrator

Why drag\drop doesn't work as Administrator mode?

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: b2210c27-5614-397a-9324-0f4f3d0de78b

item.title: Issue: [winforms-dotnet5] Move content - Printing - @adegeo
item.description:
Related to #72

When porting and migrating source content (presented as a link that goes to an article), open the source material up. Find out where it exists in the TOC. In the source project #72 navigate down to the .NET Framework 4 TOC and my notes collapsible region and expand it. Find the article title in the TOC and review the notes.

  • [ ] Printing
    • [ ] Overview (link link link link link)
    • [ ] Common tasks
      • [ ] Print a text document (mention word/pdf not supported, need 3rd party link)
      • [ ] Print a form (link)
      • [ ] Use print preview (link)

item.title: Issue: [winforms-dotnet5] Move content - Data binding - @adegeo
item.description:

When porting and migrating source content (presented as a link that goes to an article), open the source material up. Find out where it exists in the TOC. In the source project #72 navigate down to the .NET Framework 4 TOC and my notes collapsible region and expand it. Find the article title in the TOC and review the notes.

  • [ ] Data binding
    • [ ] Overview (link link link)
    • [ ] Design great data sources (link link link link)
    • [ ] Tutorial: Work with a data grid (link link)
    • [ ] Common tasks
      • [ ] Create a control binding (link link)
      • [ ] Synchronize multiple controls (link)
      • [ ] Ensure the selected row or item remains selected (Improve w/other controls like combobox/listbox link)
      • [ ] Implement the INotifyPropertyChanged interface (link)

When porting and migrating source content (presented as a link that goes to an article), open the source material up. Find out where it exists in the TOC. In the source project #72 navigate down to the .NET Framework 4 TOC and my notes collapsible region and expand it. Find the article title in the TOC and review the notes.

item.title: Issue: [winforms-dotnet5] Move content - Develop controls - @adegeo
item.description:
Related to #72

When porting and migrating source content (presented as a link that goes to an article), open the source material up. Find out where it exists in the TOC. In the source project #72 navigate down to the .NET Framework 4 TOC and my notes collapsible region and expand it. Find the article title in the TOC and review the notes.

  • [ ] Develop controls
    Custom designers for .NET require new namespace and type #41
    • [ ] Overview (link link link)
    • [ ] Design guidelines (link link link)
    • [ ] Extend controls (link)
    • Composite user controls
      • [ ] Overview (link link)
      • [ ] Constituent controls (link)
      • [ ] Tutorial: Create a user control (link)
      • Common tasks
        • [ ] Make your control invisible (link)
    • User-drawn controls
      • [ ] Overview (link)
      • [ ] Rendering an interface with OnPaint (link link)
      • [ ] Tutorial: Create a new progress control (link)
      • Common tasks
        • [ ] Create a simple control (link)
    • [ ] Visual Studio designer support
      • [ ] Overview (link link link)
      • [ ] Designer attributes (link)
      • [ ] Troubleshoot designer errors (link)
      • Common tasks
        • [ ] Manage control properties set by the designer (link)
        • [ ] Provide an image for the toolbox (link)

item.title: Issue: [winforms-dotnet5] Move content - Dialogs and popups - @adegeo
item.description:
Related to dotnet/docs-desktop#72

When porting and migrating source content (presented as a link that goes to an article), open the source material up. Find out where it exists in the TOC. In the source project #72 navigate down to the .NET Framework 4 TOC and my notes collapsible region and expand it. Find the article title in the TOC and review the notes.

  • [ ] Dialogs and popups
    This content should mirror what is in WPF. This content doesn't really exist in the .NET Framework winforms area
    • [ ] Overview (link link (WPF))
    • [ ] Common tasks
      • [ ] Display a message box (link (WPF))
      • [ ] Open a form (link (WPF))
      • [ ] Close a form (link (WPF))
      • [ ] Display a system dialog box (link (WPF) link)
      • [ ] Get or set the main form (link (WPF))

item.title: Issue: Some words missing, and typos - @ptoye
item.description:
Under 'Resources and Settings' the test says "Windows Forms projects also include Windows Form project specific files, such as Properties/Settings.settings and Properties/Resources.resx. These files may need to be migrated they are declared in your original project."

There are three problems here:

  1. The forward slashes should be backward slashes. My search for these in the old projects file failed for obvious reasons.

  2. There is a word missing after "migrated" - this is not good grammar and ambiguous. Should it be "if" or "as" or some other word?

  3. "such as" is not much help. My fairly small VB project has quite a number of references to "My Project" - which of these need migrating, and which don't?

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 98778ad1-6c20-6310-e4bc-9d9851a2270a
  • Version Independent ID: d1ef1f43-00e2-b46f-4f38-6274a34f07f6
  • Content: Migrate a Windows Forms app to .NET 5 - Windows Forms .NET
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/net/winforms/migration/index.md
  • Product: dotnet-desktop
  • Technology: dotnet-winforms
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo

item.description:
There appears to be a spelling error using then instead of than in the second paragraph of the help page: "...A TextBox requires less system resources then a RichTextBox and it is ideal when only plain text needs..."

item.title: Issue: Error on running the app - @Rolews
item.description:
Ik have followed all the steps in the tutorial, but when i run the app i get this error at line 25 'InitializeComponent();' in .cs file

"Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State
Error	CS1061	'MainWindow' does not contain a definition for 'ButtonAddName_Click' and no accessible extension method 'ButtonAddName_Click' accepting a first argument of type 'MainWindow' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)	Names	C:\Users\rolew\source\repos\Names\MainWindow.xaml	26	Active
"

Something is missing in the tutorial.

Document Details

item.title: Issue: Recursion is a bad recommendation for iterating all nodes in a TreeView - @raydixon
item.description:
This is a bad recommendation for iterating through all the nodes in a TreeView control. Depending on the size of the TreeView, you could end up with memory errors and/or crashes. A better way is to use a stack or queue and a loop. There are a lot of examples out there, but here are just a couple links to help.

  • How to traverse this tree structure without recursion c#
  • StackOverFlow on recursive iteration of TreeNodeCollection

BTW, both above posts pre-date this page.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 889e0dd8-8c1a-d612-9d9f-69793c2d34cf
  • Version Independent ID: d0b3317f-2b8c-b252-48d5-0be7cfa884c8
  • Content: Iterate Through All Nodes of TreeView Control - Windows Forms .NET Framework
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/framework/winforms/controls/how-to-iterate-through-all-nodes-of-a-windows-forms-treeview-control.md
  • Product: dotnet-framework
  • Technology: dotnet-winforms
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo

item.title: Issue: For the migration to be successful, I have to remove some data in *.resx files - @xpouyat
item.description:
For the migration of my Winforms app to be successful, I have to use Visual Studio 16.10 Preview 2 and edit resx files.

As there were error in loading some of the forms, I found that I need to remove some data in *.resx files in order for Designer to be able to load the forms without errors. The data to be removed are serialized images in the *.resx files. For example

<data name="createStreamingEndpointToolStripMenuItem.Image" type="System.Drawing.Bitmap, System.Drawing" mimetype="application/x-microsoft.net.object.bytearray.base64">
    <value>
        iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAABAAAAAQCAYAAAAf8/9hAAAABGdBTUEAALGPC/xhBQAAABl0RVh0U29m
        dHdhcmUAQWRvYmUgSW1hZ2VSZWFkeXHJZTwAAAA0SURBVDhPY/j//z9FGKsgKRhCYAcgCRjGAKMGYBqA
        rJgoPPgMwAaQNWCAUQPQDCAf/2cAAPWXSMZeEajdAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC
</value>
 </data>

So I removed all these sections in resx files and forms now load. Then I have to reconfigure the images in the menus etc.

The localization feature for winforms with .Net 5 works but I had to use Visual Studio 16.10 Preview 2. It was not working with Visual Studio 16.9.4.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 98778ad1-6c20-6310-e4bc-9d9851a2270a
  • Version Independent ID: d1ef1f43-00e2-b46f-4f38-6274a34f07f6
  • Content: Migrate a Windows Forms app to .NET 5 - Windows Forms .NET
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/net/winforms/migration/index.md
  • Product: dotnet-desktop
  • Technology: dotnet-winforms
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo

item.description:

      • [ ] Convert bound data (link)
      • [ ] Create a binding in code (link link)
      • [ ] Sort, filter, and group data with CollectionView (link link link link link)
      • [ ] Implement property change notification (link)
      • [ ] Bind to an ObservableCollection (link)
      • [ ] Bind to XML data with XPath (link link link) (should be improved)

item.title: Issue: Code listing won't work - @tom-clik
item.description:
This code listing wont' work. There is actually no reference to the Private font collection after you've added the font and you'll just get a generic font.

Not only does the listing not work but it's not obvious how to use a Private font collection after you have added it.

As far as I can tell you have to loop over the families and match the name.

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 06fc1510-cd63-95c6-f191-97cecaedd906
  • Version Independent ID: d5c0beca-4d9c-5e2b-6869-6c50e4191402
  • Content: How to: Create a Private Font Collection - Windows Forms .NET Framework
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/framework/winforms/advanced/how-to-create-a-private-font-collection.md
  • Product: dotnet-framework
  • Technology: dotnet-winforms
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo

item.title: Issue: WPF InkCanvas and DynamicRenderer - @carrilloan
item.description:
I have a wpf application in which I use an inkcanvas, I generate a video of the strokes in the inkcanvas, using the RenderTargetBitmap of the InkCanvas as frames. My problem is that when using a touch screen, the stroke of the InkCanvas is not updated until the user lifts the Stylus, and in the RenderTargetBitmap the entire stroke appears from one frame to another. I understand this is due to the DynamicRenderer. Is there a way for the InkCanvas to see the stroke as it is being made?

Document Details

⚠ Do not edit this section. It is required for docs.microsoft.com ➟ GitHub issue linking.

  • ID: 55334ea6-5b7f-9a3d-48fe-4eed4e81e9f3
  • Version Independent ID: 65ef9d14-6883-7b49-e312-80d6049ad120
  • Content: The Ink Threading Model - WPF .NET Framework
  • Content Source: dotnet-desktop-guide/framework/wpf/advanced/the-ink-threading-model.md
  • Product: dotnet-framework
  • Technology: dotnet-wpf
  • GitHub Login: @adegeo
  • Microsoft Alias: adegeo
